sections.title: Part 1
sections.passage: You will hear a conversation between two friends, Sandra and a man, about arranging activities for a family visit.

questionGroups.title: Advice on Family Visit
questionGroups.instructions: Complete the notes below. Write ONE WORD AND/OR A NUMBER for each answer.

content.text:
**Accommodation**
-

content.question: Hotel name: ______ Hotel on George Street

content.text: - cost of family room per night: £

content.question: Cost of family room per night: £ ______ (approx.)

content.text:
**Recommended Trips**

content.question: A ______ tour of the city centre (starts in Carlton Square)

content.text: - a trip by

content.question: A trip by ______ to the old fort

content.text:

- best day to visit:

content.question: Best day to visit: ______

content.text: - see the exhibition about

content.question: See the exhibition about ______ which opens soon

content.question: Save up to ______% on ticket prices at bargaintickets.com

**Free Activities**
Blakewell Gardens: Roots Music Festival

sections.title: Part 2
sections.passage: You will hear a guide talking to members of the public who are visiting a football stadium in the UK.

questionGroups.question: Which TWO things does the speaker say about visiting the football stadium with children?

options.text: Children can get their photo taken with a football player.
There is a competition for children today.
Parents must stay with their children at all times.
Children will need sunhats and drinks.
The café has a special offer on meals for children.

questionGroups.question: Which TWO features of the stadium tour are new this year?

options.text: VIP tour
360 cinema experience
audio guide
dressing room tour
tours in other languages

questionGroups.instructions: Which event in the history of football in the UK took place in each of the following years? Choose SIX answers from the box and write the correct letter, A–H, next to Questions 15–20.
questionGroups.optionsTitle: Events in the history of football

options.text: the introduction of pay for the players
a change to the design of the goal
the first use of lights for matches
the introduction of goalkeepers
the first international match
two changes to the rules of the game
the introduction of a fee for spectators
an agreement on the length of a game

sections.title: Part 3
sections.passage: You will hear two students discussing their assignment on handwriting and the impact of technology on writing.

questionGroups.question: Which TWO benefits for children of learning to write did both students find surprising?

options.text: improved fine motor skills
improved memory
improved concentration
improved imagination
improved spatial awareness

questionGroups.question: For children with dyspraxia, which TWO problems with handwriting do the students think are easiest to correct?

options.text: not spacing letters correctly
not writing in a straight line
applying too much pressure when writing
confusing letter shapes
writing very slowly

questionGroups.instructions: Choose the correct letter, A, B or C.

questions.question: What does the woman say about using laptops to teach writing to children with dyslexia?

options.text: Children often lack motivation to learn that way.
Children become fluent relatively quickly.
Children react more positively if they make a mistake.

questions.question: When discussing whether to teach cursive or print writing, the woman thinks that

options.text: cursive writing disadvantages a certain group of children.
print writing is associated with lower academic performance.
most teachers in the UK prefer a traditional approach to handwriting.

questions.question: According to the students, what impact does poor handwriting have on exam performance?

options.text: There is evidence to suggest grades are affected by poor handwriting.
Neat handwriting is less important now than it used to be.
Candidates write more slowly and produce shorter answers.

questions.question: What prediction does the man make about the future of handwriting?

options.text: Touch typing will be taught before writing by hand.
Children will continue to learn to write by hand.
People will dislike handwriting on digital devices.

questions.question: The woman is concerned that relying on digital devices has made it difficult for her to

options.text: take detailed notes.
spell and punctuate.
read old documents.

questions.question: How do the students feel about their own handwriting?

options.text: concerned they are unable to write quickly
embarrassed by comments made about it
regretful that they have lost the habit

sections.title: Part 4
sections.passage: You will hear a lecture about research conducted in the area around the Chembe Bird Sanctuary in Zambia.

questionGroups.title: Research in the area around the Chembe Bird Sanctuary
questionGroups.instructions: Complete the notes below. Write ONE WORD ONLY for each answer.

blocks.text: Research in the area around the Chembe Bird Sanctuary

content.text:
**The importance of birds of prey to the local communities**
- They destroy

content.question: They destroy ______ and other rodents

content.text: - They help to prevent farmers from being bitten by

content.question: They help to prevent farmers from being bitten by ______

content.text:
- They have been an important part of the local culture for many years.
- They now support the economy by encouraging

content.question: They now support the economy by encouraging ______ in the area

content.text:
**Falling numbers of birds of prey**
The birds may be accidentally killed:
- by

content.question: by ______ when they are hunting or sleeping

content.text: - by electrocution from contact with power lines, especially at times when there is a lot of

content.question: especially at times when there is a lot of ______

content.text: Local farmers may illegally shoot them or

content.question: Local farmers may illegally shoot them or ______ them

content.text:
**Ways of protecting chickens from birds of prey**
- clearing away vegetation from the area (unhelpful)
- providing a

content.question: providing a ______ for chickens (expensive)

content.text:
- frightening birds of prey by:
  - keeping a

content.question: keeping a ______

content.text: - making a

content.question: making a ______ - e.g. with metal objects

content.text: - A

content.question: A ______ of methods is usually most effective
